coreCompetencies: Basic chart types and when to use them (bar, line, scatter)
Notions of visual encoding: position, length, color
Builds simple charts from a prepared dataset
Applies color and labeling for readability
Uses dashboard templates in a BI tool
Notions of data-ink and avoiding chart clutter
Documents the metric and source behind a chart
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with a BI tool (Tableau, Power BI, Looker)

coreCompetencies: Chooses the right chart for the question and the data type
Builds interactive dashboards with filters and drill-downs
Prepares and shapes data for visualization
Applies grammar of graphics principles (encodings, scales)
Designs for accessibility: color-blind-safe palettes and labels
Optimizes dashboard layout for readability
Documents definitions and keeps a metric consistent
complementaryCompetencies: Notions of a charting library (D3, Vega-Lite)

coreCompetencies: Masters grammar of graphics and visual perception principles
Designs dashboards that answer specific business questions
Applies data storytelling to guide the reader to insight
Builds custom visualizations beyond default chart types
Optimizes dashboard performance (queries, extracts, caching)
Ensures accessibility and consistent visual standards
Designs geospatial and time-series visualizations
complementaryCompetencies: Creative coding with a charting library (D3, Vega)
Notions of a semantic layer for consistent metrics
indicators: Designs visualization systems for a team

coreCompetencies: Defines the visualization strategy and design system for a domain
Standardizes chart patterns, palettes and accessibility guidelines
Architects self-service dashboards and certified metrics
Optimizes performance across large dashboards and datasets
Leads data storytelling for executive and operational audiences
Manages the semantic layer for consistent, reusable metrics
Reduces cognitive load through layout and interaction design
complementaryCompetencies: Design tokens and reusable visualization components
indicators: Defines visualization standards for the organization
Leads a visualization or BI team

coreCompetencies: Leads the enterprise visualization platform and design system

Governs metric definitions and certified dashboards
Sets accessibility and performance standards at scale
Establishes data storytelling practices and templates
Defines interaction patterns for human-data exploration
Runs visualization enablement and training programs

Manages the design system and self-service platform at scale
